perturbation.original_text: b) "SERVICES PERIOD" means that period of time commencing upon the full execution of this Agreement by both Parties and terminating on May 31, 2014 unless sooner terminated under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: b) "SERVICES PERIOD" means that period of time commencing upon the full execution of this Agreement by both Parties and terminating on May 31, 2013 unless sooner terminated under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the SERVICES PERIOD to end in 2013 instead of 2014, it creates a contradiction with Paragraph 4(b) which states payments will be made until June 1, 2015. This creates an in-text contradiction because Berkshire is agreeing to payment terms that extend beyond the stated service period. The contradiction emerges because the payment structure is tied to the original service period, and the altered end date now creates ambiguity about the payment obligations after May 31, 2013.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) In consideration of the rights and benefits granted to Berkshire hereunder, Berkshire shall pay to Auriemma the sum of Four Hundred Eighty Thousand and 00/100 Dollars ($480,000.00), as set forth in Paragraph 4 (b)-(c) below. (b) Berkshire will make four (4) equal cash payments to Auriemma in the amount of Ninety Thousand and 00/100 Dollars ($90,000.00) on June 1, 2012, June 1, 2013, June 1, 2014, and June 1, 2015. (c) Berkshire will cause to be issued to Auriemma on June 1, 2012, June 1, 2013, June 1, 2014, and June 1, 2015, (each an "issuance date") four (4) blocks of shares of unrestricted common stock of Berkshire's parent corporation, Berkshire Hills Bancorp, Inc. (BHLB), each of which as of its respective issuance date shall have a value of Thirty Thousand and 00/100 Dollars ($30,000.00), based upon the closing price of BHLB common shares at the end of the last trading day immediately preceding the issuance date.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the "SERVICES PERIOD" to end in 2013, while Paragraph 4(b) specifies payments until June 1, 2015. This creates a direct in-text contradiction as Berkshire is obligated to make payments beyond the stated service period. The altered end date creates ambiguity and unenforceability regarding the payment obligations after May 31, 2013, as the original agreement ties payments to the service period. This falls under the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: c) The License Rights are exclusive to Berkshire and may not be assigned or in any way conveyed by Berkshire without Auriemma's express written consent, except in the event of a merger by Berkshire with another entity offering Banking Services.
perturbation.changed_text: c) The License Rights are exclusive to Berkshire and may not be assigned or in any way conveyed by Berkshire without Auriemma's express written consent.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the exception regarding mergers with other banking entities, it creates an inconsistency with Section 22, which discusses assignment. Section 22 allows for assignment under specific conditions (reorganization, merger, consolidation, or sale of assets). Removing the merger exception in Section 2c makes it contradict with Section 22 where merger assignement is allowed. Now, if Berkshire merges, it's unclear if the License Rights can be transferred without Auriemma's consent, creating legal ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall bind and inure to the benefit of Auriemma and his successors and permitted assigns. Nothing herein shall prevent Auriemma from assigning the monetary benefits (but not the obligations) of this Agreement, as he may so desire. Berkshire may not assign this Agreement, in whole or in part, without Auriemma's written consent. In the case of reorganization, merger, consolidation, or sale of all or substantially all of its assets, any attempt to assign this Agreement other than as permitted above will be null and void.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly allowed assignment of License Rights in the event of a merger, even without Auriemma's consent. The changed text removes this exception, now requiring Auriemma's consent for assignment in all cases, including mergers. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines specific conditions under which Berkshire can assign the agreement (including mergers). The change now creates a scenario where Section 2(c) requires consent for merger-related assignments, while the contradicted section suggests assignment is permissible under certain merger conditions. This creates a direct and impactful contradiction regarding assignment rights.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Auriemma will be available one (1) time per calendar quarter during the Services Period to meet and address Berkshire's small business customer groups.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Auriemma will be available one (1) time during the Services Period to meet and address Berkshire's small business customer groups.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'per calendar quarter' specification, it creates ambiguity regarding the frequency of Auriemma's obligation to meet with Berkshire's small business customer groups, and it creates a contradiction with the description of services in 'SCHEDULE A'. This creates an in-text contradiction since the services description details the availability of Auriemma one time per calendar quarter, and this modification does not provide the correct frequency.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Auriemma will act as the spokesperson for Berkshire and its Affiliates. 2. Auriemma will participate in one (1) recording session annually during the Services Period of not more than two (2) hours, not including travel time, to record a radio advertising spot at a date and location to be mutually agreed upon; 3. Auriemma will participate in one (1) production session annually during the Services Period of not more than three (3) hours, not including travel time, to record a television advertising spot at a date and location to be mutually agreed upon; 4. Auriemma will participate in one (1) photo session annually during the Services Period of not more than two (2) hours, not including travel time, at a date and location to be mutually agreed upon; 5. Auriemma will be available for two (2) appearances annually during the Services Period within the Contract Territory, the date and location to be mutually agreed upon, each not more than one (1) hour in duration, where Auriemma will meet, greet and pose for photos. 6. Auriemma will be available one (1) time during the Services Period to meet and address Berkshire's small business customer groups. 7. Auriemma will sign 100 items per year during the Services Period for Berkshire provided all items are to be obtained at Berkshire's cost, provided, however, that Berkshire shall not sell any items autographed by Auriemma and Auriemma will not be required to sign any item that is manufactured by a competitor of Nike and subject to Nike's right to disapprove any items. 8. Auriemma will permit Berkshire to place a link on its website (Berkshirebank.com) throughout the Contract Period, directing website viewers to a landing page promoting Berkshire's product or brand containing the Auriemma Identification. 9. Berkshire may utilize Auriemma and the Auriemma Identification throughout the Contract Period in all approved materials including billboards, subway/bus ads, direct mail, stand-ups, counter cards, posters, etc.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies Auriemma's availability 'one (1) time per calendar quarter'. The changed text removes 'per calendar quarter', making it 'one (1) time during the Services Period'. The contradicted text is a schedule that lists all of Auriemma's services, including the 'one (1) time during the Services Period to meet and address Berkshire's small business customer groups.' By removing the 'per calendar quarter' specification, the changed text directly contradicts the intended frequency outlined in the schedule of services, specifically item 6. The altered text removes key information about frequency of availability, creating ambiguity and directly conflicting with the clear, periodic nature of the obligation as initially defined, creating an in-text contradiction.
